title: LaurentLogExpansion
summary: Models a finite Laurent/log expansion, parity conditions, coefficient support, and logarithmic terms.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Models a finite Laurent/log expansion, parity conditions, coefficient support, and logarithmic terms.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Renormalization/LaurentLogExpansion.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A Laurent expansion with one logarithmic coefficient and finite support. Its mathematical data are: coeff: Int maps to R; log Coeff: R; lower Bound: Int; upper Bound: Int; lower le upper: lower Bound < equals upper Bound; coeff vanish lt: for every m : Int, m < lower Bound maps to coeff m equals 0; coeff vanish gt: for every m : Int, upper Bound < m maps to coeff m equals 0.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{coeff}&\colon \mathbb{Z} \longrightarrow R\\\operatorname{logCoeff}&\colon R\\\operatorname{lowerBound}&\colon \mathbb{Z}\\\operatorname{upperBound}&\colon \mathbb{Z}\\\operatorname{lower\_le\_upper}&\colon \operatorname{lowerBound} <= \operatorname{upperBound}\\\operatorname{coeff\_vanish\_lt}&\colon \forall m \colon \mathbb{Z}, m < \operatorname{lowerBound} \longrightarrow \operatorname{coeff} m = 0\\\operatorname{coeff\_vanish\_gt}&\colon \forall m \colon \mathbb{Z}, \operatorname{upperBound} < m \longrightarrow \operatorname{coeff} m = 0\end{aligned}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The finite interval containing every potentially nonzero Laurent term. In explicit terms, its defining expression is Finset.Icc E.lower Bound E.upper Bound.
For m in the integers, and hm of type m < E.lower Bound, E.coeff m equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem coeff_eq_zero_of_lt {m : Int} (hm : m < E.lowerBound) : E.coeff m = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For m in the integers, and hm of type E.upper Bound < m, E.coeff m equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem coeff_eq_zero_of_gt {m : Int} (hm : E.upperBound < m) : E.coeff m = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Parity through order N, measured by the absolute exponent. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, its defining expression is for every m : Int, m.nat Abs < equals N maps to Odd m maps to E.coeff m equals 0.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{EvenExpansionToOrder}(N)=\forall m \colon \mathbb{Z}, m.\operatorname{natAbs} <= N \longrightarrow \operatorname{Odd} m \longrightarrow E.\operatorname{coeff} m = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Odd parity through order N, measured by the absolute exponent. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, its defining expression is for every m : Int, m.nat Abs < equals N maps to Even m maps to E.coeff m equals 0.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{OddExpansionToOrder}(N)=\forall m \colon \mathbb{Z}, m.\operatorname{natAbs} <= N \longrightarrow \operatorname{Even} m \longrightarrow E.\operatorname{coeff} m = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Top-level spelling of even parity for renormalization applications. In explicit terms, for r a type, e of type laurent log expansion r, and n in the natural numbers, its defining expression is E.Even Expansion To Order N.
correspondence.leanType: def EvenExpansionToOrder {R : Type u} [Zero R] (E : LaurentLogExpansion R) (N : Nat) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Top-level spelling of odd parity for renormalization applications. In explicit terms, for r a type, e of type laurent log expansion r, and n in the natural numbers, its defining expression is E.Odd Expansion To Order N.
correspondence.leanType: def OddExpansionToOrder {R : Type u} [Zero R] (E : LaurentLogExpansion R) (N : Nat) : Prop

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
